mnemonics.title: FRAME ALLOCATION TYPES
mnemonics.content: EQUAL P'S: EQUAL, PROPORTIONAL, PRIORITY, LOCAL, GLOBAL - FIVE TYPES TO MEMORIZE

mnemonics.title: WORKING SET KEY IDEAS
mnemonics.content: W(t,w) = WINDOW SIZE - KEEPS TRACK OF RECENT REFERENCES - WINDOW TOO SMALL = TOO MANY FAULTS; WINDOW TOO BIG = WASTED MEMORY

acronyms.usage: PFF monitoring allocates more frames when page fault rate exceeds upper threshold and removes frames when below lower threshold
Page replacement algorithm that removes the oldest page in memory, commonly used with frame allocation strategies
Replacement algorithm that evicts the page least recently accessed, often combined with global frame allocation
Set of pages a process has referenced during time interval Δ, used to determine appropriate frame allocation
Hardware cache that stores recent page-table entries, reducing memory access time for frame lookup

visualTips.visualization: Think of a pizza: EQUAL = each person gets same number of slices regardless of hunger; PROPORTIONAL = slices based on how hungry each person is (their size/priority)
Global = shared buffet where anyone can take any dish; Local = each person only can take from their own assigned platters
Imagine trying to work at a desk with all your files in different storage rooms - you spend more time fetching files than doing work
Think of a student's open books on a desk - these are the pages in the working set; closed books in the shelf are pages not currently needed

analogies.analogy: Like a teacher distributing limited notebooks among students - equal gives everyone same number, proportional gives based on need, priority gives more to important students
Like a library where any student can take any book - efficient but one student might take all books, causing others to wait
Like each student has their own shelf - they can only use their own books, even if another student has unused books on their shelf
Like a chef keeping only the ingredients for current recipes on the counter - only what's actively being used stays within easy reach
